evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Batch to my Cordova app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to track EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and log each tracked barcode's data to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes a complete BarcodeBatch integration into EmptyApp.js inside the deviceready handler. It includes context creation via Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize, camera setup using Scandit.BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings(), BarcodeBatchSettings with EAN-13 and Code 128 enabled, new Scandit.BarcodeBatch(settings) + context.setMode, a didUpdateSession listener that logs tracked barcodes, DataCaptureView.forContext + connectToElement, camera on, and barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true. Shows the setup checklist and uses Scandit.* global namespace throughout.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions cordova plugin add scandit-cordova-datacapture-core and scandit-cordova-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions cordova prepare after plugin install
Setup checklist mentions camera permissions are auto-configured by the plugins
document.addEventListener('deviceready' is used as the init gate
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
Scandit.BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is called
Scandit.Camera.withSettings( is called
context.setFrameSource( is called
new Scandit.BarcodeBatchSettings() is present
Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Scandit.Symbology.Code128 is enabled
new Scandit.BarcodeBatch(settings) is present (context NOT passed to BarcodeBatch constructor)
context.setMode( is called with barcodeBatch
barcodeBatch.addListener( or window.barcodeBatch.addListener( is called with a didUpdateSession callback
session.trackedBarcodes is accessed inside the didUpdateSession callback
Scandit.DataCaptureView.forContext( is present
connectToElement( is called on the view
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true is present
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.On) is called
import statements from scandit-cordova-datacapture-* are NOT present in runtime code (Scandit.* global is used instead)

evals.prompt: I want to vary the highlight color for tracked barcodes based on symbology in my MatrixScan Batch Cordova app (IntegratedApp.js). EAN-13 barcodes should show green, all others red. Use BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay with brushForTrackedBarcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a listener on window.basicOverlay (or a new BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay) with a brushForTrackedBarcode callback that returns a green Brush for EAN13UPCA and a red Brush for all other symbologies. Adds a note that brushForTrackedBarcode requires the MatrixScan AR add-on. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: basicOverlay.listener = { is set with a brushForTrackedBarcode callback
brushForTrackedBarcode checks trackedBarcode.barcode.symbology === Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA (or equivalent)
A green-ish Brush is returned for EAN13UPCA
A red-ish (or different) Brush is returned as default / for other symbologies
new Scandit.Brush( is used to construct at least one brush
A comment or note mentions that brushForTrackedBarcode requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan Batch app (IntegratedApp.js), hide the highlight for barcodes whose data starts with '0'. All other barcodes should keep their default highlight.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a brushForTrackedBarcode listener on basicOverlay that returns a transparent Brush (or null) for barcodes whose data starts with '0', and returns the default brush (or null for the listener to fall back to the overlay brush) for all others. Mentions AR add-on requirement.

assertions.text: brushForTrackedBarcode callback is set on basicOverlay.listener
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is checked for startsWith('0') or equivalent prefix check
A transparent brush or null is returned for barcodes starting with '0'
The transparent brush has zero or near-zero alpha, OR null is returned (semantic)
A non-null brush or the overlay's default brush is returned for other barcodes
A comment or note mentions that brushForTrackedBarcode requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add AR bubble annotations above each tracked barcode in my Cordova MatrixScan Batch app (IntegratedApp.js). Each bubble should show the barcode's data. Use BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay using new Scandit.BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay(barcodeBatch), sets a listener with anchorForTrackedBarcode (Anchor.TopCenter) and offsetForTrackedBarcode (0, -1 Fraction), calls view.addOverlay with the advanced overlay, and inside didUpdateSession calls advancedOverlay.setViewForTrackedBarcode with a Scandit.TrackedBarcodeView built from a DOM element showing the barcode data. Notes that this requires the MatrixScan AR add-on.

assertions.text: new Scandit.BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay( is constructed with barcodeBatch
view.addOverlay( is called with the advanced overlay
advancedOverlay.listener is set with anchorForTrackedBarcode and offsetForTrackedBarcode callbacks
anchorForTrackedBarcode returns Scandit.Anchor.TopCenter or an equivalent anchor
offsetForTrackedBarcode returns a PointWithUnit with a negative vertical offset using MeasureUnit.Fraction
Scandit.TrackedBarcodeView.withHTMLElement( is called to create the serialized view
advancedOverlay.setViewForTrackedBarcode( is called with the TrackedBarcodeView and trackedBarcode
The bubble DOM element shows the barcode data (trackedBarcode.barcode.data) (semantic)
A comment or note mentions that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add lifecycle management to my MatrixScan Batch Cordova app (IntegratedApp.js) so that scanning pauses when the app goes to the background and resumes when it comes back to the foreground.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds document.addEventListener('pause', ...) and document.addEventListener('resume', ...) handlers that set barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false and switch the camera off on pause, and set barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true and switch the camera on on resume.

assertions.text: document.addEventListener('pause', is registered
document.addEventListener('resume', is registered
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false or window.barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false is called in the pause handler
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the pause handler
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true or window.barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true is called in the resume handler
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.On) is called in the resume handler
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Show me how to properly clean up MatrixScan Batch in my Cordova app (IntegratedApp.js) when the user navigates away from the scanning screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill provides a teardown function (or augments the existing uninitialize function) that switches the camera off, sets barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false, and calls view.detachFromElement(). Explains when to call it.

assertions.text: camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the teardown function
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false is set in the teardown function
view.detachFromElement() is called in the teardown function
The teardown function is explained as being called on navigation away or screen exit (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Cordova app (IntegratedApp.js), the tracked barcodes are currently shown with a rectangular frame. I want a round dot highlight on each tracked barcode instead.
evals.expected_output: The skill changes the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay style from Frame to Dot — either by passing Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot as the second constructor argument to new Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay, or by reconstructing the overlay with that style. The rest of the integration (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view, addOverlay) is preserved.

assertions.text: Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot is used as the overlay style
Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame is NOT used as the overlay style in the final code
new Scandit.BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay( is constructed with window.barcodeBatch and the Dot style
window.view.addOverlay( is called with the basic overlay
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan Batch app (IntegratedApp.js), when the user taps a tracked barcode's highlight I want to log its data and symbology to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a listener on the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay with a didTapTrackedBarcode callback that reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data and trackedBarcode.barcode.symbology and logs them. Notes that the basic overlay listener requires the MatrixScan AR add-on. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: basicOverlay.listener is set with a didTapTrackedBarcode callback
didTapTrackedBarcode receives (overlay, trackedBarcode) and reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data
trackedBarcode.barcode.symbology is read inside the didTapTrackedBarcode callback
The tap callback is named exactly didTapTrackedBarcode (not didTapViewForTrackedBarcode, which is the advanced-overlay callback)
A comment or note mentions that the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay listener requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I added AR bubbles with BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay to my Cordova MatrixScan Batch app (IntegratedApp.js). Now I want to handle taps on those AR bubble views so I can open a details screen for the tapped barcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay (if not present) and sets a didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback on advancedOverlay.listener that reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data and performs the details action. It uses didTapViewForTrackedBarcode (the advanced-overlay tap callback, which Cordova supports) — NOT didTapTrackedBarcode (the basic-overlay callback). Notes the MatrixScan AR add-on requirement.

assertions.text: new Scandit.BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay( is constructed with window.barcodeBatch
advancedOverlay.listener is set with a didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback
didTapViewForTrackedBarcode receives (overlay, trackedBarcode) and reads trackedBarcode.barcode.data
The tap callback is named exactly didTapViewForTrackedBarcode (not didTapTrackedBarcode, which is the basic-overlay callback)
window.view.addOverlay( is called with the advanced overlay
A comment or note mentions that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan Batch app (IntegratedApp.js) I keep a per-barcode highlight count in a plain object keyed by tracked-barcode identifier. When a barcode leaves the frame I need to delete its entry so the object doesn't leak. How do I detect barcodes that were removed this frame?
evals.expected_output: The skill uses session.removedTrackedBarcodes inside the didUpdateSession callback to get the identifiers of barcodes lost this frame, and deletes the corresponding entries from the app-side map. It clarifies that removedTrackedBarcodes is an array of identifier strings (not TrackedBarcode objects), and warns not to hold session references outside the callback. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed inside the didUpdateSession callback
session.removedTrackedBarcodes is iterated (forEach or for-of) to handle each removed identifier
removedTrackedBarcodes is treated as an array of identifier strings, not TrackedBarcode objects (the entries are used directly as keys, not as .barcode.data) (semantic)
The per-barcode entry is deleted from the app-side map using the removed identifier as the key
A note warns not to hold references to session or its arrays outside the didUpdateSession callback (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan Batch app (IntegratedApp.js) I want a beep and vibration each time a NEW barcode starts being tracked. BarcodeBatch doesn't seem to beep on its own. How do I add that?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeBatch has no built-in/automatic feedback (unlike BarcodeCapture or BarcodeCount, BarcodeBatch has no feedback property), so feedback must be emitted manually. It constructs a Scandit.Feedback from Scandit.Vibration.defaultVibration and Scandit.Sound.defaultSound (or uses Scandit.Feedback.defaultFeedback), stores it at module/window scope, and calls feedback.emit() inside didUpdateSession for each entry in session.addedTrackedBarcodes (the new-this-frame barcodes), so the beep fires once per newly tracked barcode rather than every frame. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: The skill states that BarcodeBatch has no automatic/built-in feedback and that feedback must be emitted manually (semantic)
A Scandit.Feedback is created via new Scandit.Feedback(Scandit.Vibration.defaultVibration, Scandit.Sound.defaultSound) OR via Scandit.Feedback.defaultFeedback
feedback.emit() is called to play the sound and vibration
feedback.emit() is called for entries in session.addedTrackedBarcodes (so it fires once per newly tracked barcode, not every frame) (semantic)
The skill does NOT set a barcodeBatch.feedback property (BarcodeBatch has no such property — feedback is emitted manually) (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)
